CG.s: Oil-dependent economie ($15.7B BBP) with 34.74% combined oil and gas rents — one of Sub-Saharan Africa's highest. TotalEnergies and Eni are major operators. Governance challenges with long-ruling president (Sassou Nguesso since 1997). Significant tropical forest cover.

sectors.w: Congo-Brazzaville's oil production is in long-term decline from mature offshore fields. As extraction costs rise and reserves deplete, the overlappend belang between international operators and government revenue verzwakt — diversification becomes necessary.
bp: Redirect oil revenues to economic diversification (forestry, agriculture, services) through transparent budget allocation. Wanneer non-oil sectors generate more employment and revenue, het overlappende belang in continued extraction from depleting fields breaks.
Implementeren EITI-standard per-block revenue and emissies disclosure. Congo joined EITI in 2004 — extending to milieu- data makes W computable per production unit.
Niet breekbaar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report milieu- violations at oil facilities to the Ministry of Environment
Document revenue discrepancies — report to EITI Congo multi-stakeholder group
Report concerns to international operator compliance channels (TotalEnergies, Eni)
TotalEnergies, Eni: Implementeren Paris-aligned transition plans for Congo operations
SNPC: Ontwikkelen economic diversification strategy using petroleum revenues
Aannemen per-block emissies reporting standards
Ministry of Hydrocarbons: Versterken milieu- conditions in production sharing contracts
EITI Congo: Expand reporting to include milieu- impact data
Central Bank (BEAC): Integrate resource revenue volatility risk into macroprudential supervision
Pleiten via CEMAC for regional milieu- standards in oil and gas extraction
Support EITI enhanced reporting with milieu- metrics
Aandringen op Central African Forest Initiative (CAFI) enforcement on forest protection as alternative revenue source
Leverage UNFCCC mechanisms for fossil fuel transition support
i: Ministère de l'Environnement, du Développement Durable et du Bassin du Congo — the environment ministry. SNPC (Société Nationale des Pétroles du Congo) — the national oil company. EITI Congo — the Republic of Congo has been an EITI member since 2004. Note: Whistleblower protections are minimal under current governance.

TotalEnergies — the largest oil operator in Congo-Brazzaville, operating the Moho Nord deepwater project and other fields. Eni — operates significant offshore concessions. SNPC (Société Nationale des Pétroles du Congo) — the national oil company holding state equity in all production sharing contracts.

Ministère des Hydrocarbures — oversees oil and gas policy and licensing. BEAC (Banque des États de l'Afrique Centrale) — the central bank of the CEMAC monetary zone (shared with Cameroon, CAR, Chad, Equatorial Guinea, Gabon). EITI Congo — publishes annual reports on extractive industry revenues.

Note: Congo-Brazzaville under President Sassou Nguesso is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. The Assemblée nationale (151 members) and Sénat (72 members) do not function as independent democratic institutions. International engagement channels: CEMAC (Communauté Économique et Monétaire de l'Afrique Centrale), African Union, CAFI (Central African Forest Initiative) — a partnership between Central African countries and donors to protect tropical forests.

CI.s: Largest economie in francophone West Africa ($87.1B BBP). World's largest cocoa producer (~45% of global supply). Agriculture is 15.9% of BBP with cocoa, coffee, and palm oil as main cash crops. Growing oil production (oil=0.7%). Rapid economic growth (~7% annual average).

sectors.w: Côte d'Ivoire's cocoa monoculture has driven 80%+ of original forest loss. EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR) will require ontbossing-free supply chains — when per-farm ontbossing data is linked to cocoa transactions, W wordt berekenbaar.
bp: EU Deforestation Regulation compliance forces traceability to farm level. Wanneer cocoa buyers must verify zero-ontbossing, the overlappend belang between traders and smallholders in clearing forest for new plantations breaks — agroforestry becomes the viable model.

Mandatory per-farm milieu- data (tree cover, soil carbon, water use) linked to cocoa lot traceability. The Cocoa and Forests Initiative already creates a framework — extending to mandatory data makes W computable from cocoa prices.

Gedeeltelijk breekbaar. Cocoa monoculture inherently reduces biodiversity compared to native forest. However, cocoa agroforestry systems can maintain significant tree cover and biodiversity — Côte d'Ivoire's National REDD+ Strategy includes agroforestry targets.
a: Report illegal ontbossing for cocoa expansion to SODEFOR (Société de Développement des Forêts) or OIPR (Office Ivoirien des Parcs et Réserves)
Document child labor in cocoa production — report to CNS (Comité National de Surveillance des actions de lutte contre le travail des enfants)
Report pesticide misuse — notify ANADER (Agence Nationale d'Appui au Développement Rural)
Cocoa traders (Cargill, Barry Callebaut, Olam): Implementeren full farm-level traceability ahead of EUDR deadline
Investeren in cocoa agroforestry systems that maintain tree cover while improving yields
Aannemen living income reference price commitments for cocoa farmers
CCC (Conseil du Café-Cacao): Handhaven traceability requirements and link farmgate prices to milieu- compliance
MINEDD (Ministry of Environment): Versterken enforcement against ontbossing in classified forests
ANADER: Scale agroforestry extension programs to all cocoa-growing regions
Neem contact op met uw Député in the Assemblée nationale om te eisen enforcement of the Forest Code and protection of classified forests
Support EU Deforestation Regulation implementation with adequate transition support for smallholder farmers
Pleiten via the Cocoa and Forests Initiative for binding ontbossing targets
Aandringen op living income benchmarks in international cocoa pricing through ICCO
i: SODEFOR (Société de Développement des Forêts) — the state forestry company responsible for managing classified forests, many of which have been illegally cleared for cocoa. OIPR (Office Ivoirien des Parcs et Réserves) — manages national parks and protected areas. CNS (Comité National de Surveillance) — the national committee monitoring child labor in cocoa, coordinated with the International Cocoa Initiative (ICI). ANADER — the national rural development support agency providing agricultural extension services.

Major cocoa traders operating in Côte d'Ivoire: Cargill, Barry Callebaut, Olam International, Sucden. These companies intermediate the majority of Ivorian cocoa exports. Cocoa and Forests Initiative (CFI) — a public-private partnership between cocoa-producing countries (Côte d'Ivoire, Ghana) and leading chocolate companies to end ontbossing in cocoa supply chains. The Conseil du Café-Cacao (CCC) — the Ivorian cocoa board that sets farmgate prices and regulates the sector.

CCC (Conseil du Café-Cacao) — the government body regulating Côte d'Ivoire's cocoa and coffee sectors, setting minimum farmgate prices, managing quality standards, and overseeing exports. MINEDD (Ministère de l'Environnement et du Développement Durable) — the environment ministry responsible for forest protection, climate policy, and milieu- regulation.

Assemblée nationale de Côte d'Ivoire — unicameral parliament with 255 members. Citizens can contact deputies through the official Assembly website. Sénat — the upper house (99 members, established 2020). ICCO (International Cocoa Organization) — the intergovernmental body coordinating global cocoa policy, headquartered in Abidjan. EU Deforestation Regulation (EUDR, 2023/1115) — requires EU importers to verify that commodities including cocoa are not linked to ontbossing after December 2020.

CL.s: Upper-middle-income economie ($330.3B BBP) with massive mijnbouw sector (16.23% of BBP). World's largest copper producer (~27% of global supply) and second-largest lithium producer. Strong institutions and democratic governance. Renewable energy leader in Latin America (solar in Atacama).

sectors.w: Chile's copper and lithium mijnbouw depletes water resources in the Atacama Desert (one of Earth's driest regions). Wanneer per mijn water consumption data is published alongside production revenue, W wordt berekenbaar from mijnbouw output data.
bp: Implementeren water pricing that reflects true scarcity in mijnbouw regions. Wanneer miners pay the real cost of water extraction in hyper-arid zones, het overlappende belang in water-intensive extraction methods verzwakt — desalination and dry processing become economically preferred.

Mandatory per mijn water balance, tailings chemistry, and emissies disclosure. Chile's SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente) already collects milieu- data — making it publicly accessible per mine makes W computable.
Gedeeltelijk breekbaar. Mining inherently disturbs landscapes. However, dry processing, desalination, and tailings reprocessing can significantly reduce water dependence and waste generation.
a: Report milieu- violations to SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente)
Document water rights violations — report to DGA (Dirección General de Aguas)
Use Chile's klokkenluider law (Ley 21.015) and milieu- complaint channels (denuncia ambiental) for protected reporting
CODELCO (state copper company): Transition to desalinated water for all operations
Aannemen Science Based Targets for emissies reduction across mijnbouw operations
Implementeren ICMM Mining Principles including tailings management standards
SMA: Increase milieu- monitoring frequency and enforce water extraction limits at mijnbouw operations
Sernageomin: Versterken tailings dam safety standards (Chile has 740+ tailings facilities)
Ministry of Mining: Implementeren the National Lithium Strategy with binding milieu- standards
Neem contact op met uw Diputado or Senador in the Congreso Nacional om te eisen mandatory water-neutral mijnbouw operations through desalination requirements
Support the Escazú Agreement on milieu- access rights in Latin America (Chile has not ratified)
Advocate for OECD Due Diligence Guidance enforcement for mijnbouw supply chains
Aandringen op international coordination on critical mineral sustainability standards through the Minerals Security Partnership
i: SMA (Superintendencia del Medio Ambiente) — Chile's milieu- enforcement agency, responsible for monitoring compliance with milieu- permits (RCA — Resolución de Calificación Ambiental). SMA can impose fines up to $8.2M and order facility closure. DGA (Dirección General de Aguas) — the national water authority managing water rights and monitoring extraction. SEA (Servicio de Evaluación Ambiental) — conducts milieu- impact assessments for mijnbouw projects.

CODELCO (Corporación Nacional del Cobre) — Chile's staatsbedrijf copper company and the world's largest copper producer. CODELCO operates Chuquicamata, El Teniente, and other major mines. SQM (Sociedad Química y Minera de Chile) — one of the world's largest lithium producers, operating in the Salar de Atacama. Albemarle — US company operating lithium extraction in the Salar de Atacama alongside SQM. BHP, Anglo American — major international copper mijnbouw companies operating in Chile.

Sernageomin (Servicio Nacional de Geología y Minería) — the national geological and mijnbouw service, responsible for mine safety, tailings dam oversight, and geological surveys. Chile has over 740 tailings facilities, the largest inventory in the world. Ministry of Mining — oversees mijnbouw policy including the 2023 National Lithium Strategy, which established state-led lithium development through public-private partnerships.

Congreso Nacional de Chile — bicameral legislature consisting of the Cámara de Diputadas y Diputados (155 members) and the Senado (50 members). Citizens can contact representatives through camara.cl and senado.cl. Chile is an OECD member with strong democratic institutions. Escazú Agreement — the Regional Agreement on Access to Information, Public Participation and Justice in Environmental Matters in Latin America and the Caribbean. Chile signed but has not ratified.

CM.s: Central African economie ($53.3B BBP) with oil production (2.94% combined rents) and significant agriculture (18.5% BBP — cocoa, palm oil, timber). Forest cover declining due to logging and agricultural expansion. Governance challenges and Anglophone crisis.

sectors.w: Cameroon's oil production is declining from mature fields. As extraction costs rise, the overlappend belang between operators (Perenco, Addax) and government revenue verzwakt — diversification to sustainable forestry and agriculture becomes economically rational.
bp: Redirect declining oil revenues to sustainable forest management and agricultural diversification. Cameroon's forests are a carbon sink worth more standing than cleared. Wanneer REDD+ payments exceed marginal oil revenue, het overlappende belang in continued extraction breaks.
Implementeren EITI-standard per-block milieu- disclosure. Cameroon's EITI membership provides the framework — extending to milieu- data makes W computable.
Niet breekbaar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report milieu- violations to MINEPDED (Ministry of Environment, Nature Protection and Sustainable Ontwikkelenment)
Document illegal logging or land seizures — report to international forestry monitoring organizations (Global Forest Watch)
Report through EITI Cameroon multi-stakeholder channels
Perenco, Addax/Sinopec: Implementeren milieu- monitoring and emissies reporting for all Cameroon operations
SNH: Ontwikkelen post-oil diversification strategy
Aannemen TCFD-aligned climate disclosure
MINMIDT (Ministry of Mines): Versterken milieu- conditions in production licenses
MINEPDED: Handhaven milieu- impact assessment requirements
EITI Cameroon: Expand reporting to milieu- data
Neem contact op met uw Député in the Assemblée nationale om te eisen transparent management of declining oil revenues
Support CAFI (Central African Forest Initiative) for forest conservation as alternative revenue
Pleiten via CEMAC for regional milieu- standards
Aandringen op EITI enhanced reporting
i: MINEPDED (Ministère de l'Environnement, de la Protection de la Nature et du Développement Durable) — Cameroon's environment ministry. SNH (Société Nationale des Hydrocarbures) — the national hydrocarbons company managing state interests in oil and gas. EITI Cameroon — member since 2007. Note: Whistleblower protections are limited.

Perenco — the largest oil producer in Cameroon. Addax Petroleum (Sinopec subsidiary) — operates offshore concessions. SNH manages the state's petroleum interests and holds equity in production sharing contracts.

MINMIDT (Ministère des Mines, de l'Industrie et du Développement Technologique) — oversees mijnbouw and industrial policy. CONAC (Commission Nationale Anti-Corruption) — Cameroon's anti-corruption commission.

Assemblée nationale du Cameroun — 180 members. Sénat — 100 members. Note: Cameroon's democratic institutions are constrained under long-ruling President Paul Biya (in power since 1982). International channels: CEMAC, African Union, CAFI.

CO.s: Major Latin American economie ($418.8B BBP) with significant oil (3.42% rents) and coal (0.73% rents) production. Ecopetrol is the largest company. Biodiversity hotspot (most biodiverse country per square kilometer). 2016 peace agreement with FARC opened new governance challenges in former conflict zones.

sectors.w: Colombia's current administration (Petro, 2022-) has announced a policy of no new oil exploration licenses. If maintained, het overlappende belang in fossil fuel expansion breaks as existing fields deplete. Colombia's renewable potential (hydro, solar, wind) provides viable alternatives.
bp: Maintain and implement the no-new-exploration-licenses policy while investing transition revenues in hernieuwbare energie. Wanneer energy investment flows shift from fossil to renewable, het overlappende belang in continued exploration breaks.

Mandatory per veld emissies disclosure for all Ecopetrol and international operator fields. Colombia's ANH (Agencia Nacional de Hidrocarburos) already collects production data — extending to emissies makes W computable.
Niet breekbaar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report milieu- violations to ANLA (Autoridad Nacional de Licencias Ambientales)
Document community health impacts near oil operations — report to Defensoría del Pueblo
Report through Ecopetrol's ethics line or international operator compliance channels
Ecopetrol: Accelerate energy transition strategy with binding renewable targets
Implementeren TCFD-aligned reporting across all operations
Investeren in hernieuwbare energie in post-conflict zones to provide economic alternatives
MinAmbiente: Versterken milieu- licensing for extractive projects in biodiverse regions
CREG: Hervormen energy tariffs to accelerate hernieuwbare energie deployment
ANH: Implementeren per veld emissies monitoring
Neem contact op met uw Representante or Senador in the Congreso to support the energy transition framework legislation
Versterken Colombia's NDC with binding sectoral emissies reduction targets
Leverage OECD membership to advance milieu- governance standards
Support the Leticia Pact for Amazon conservation as alternative economic model
i: ANLA (Autoridad Nacional de Licencias Ambientales) — Colombia's milieu- licensing authority, responsible for milieu- impact assessment and compliance monitoring for extractive projects. Defensoría del Pueblo — the national human rights ombudsman. Contraloría General de la República — the national audit office overseeing public resource management including oil revenues.

Ecopetrol — Colombia's largest company (88.5% staatsbedrijf), responsible for most domestic oil production. Ecopetrol has announced energy transition targets but continues significant upstream investment. ANH (Agencia Nacional de Hidrocarburos) — manages exploration and production contracts.

MinAmbiente (Ministerio de Ambiente y Desarrollo Sostenible) — Colombia's environment ministry. CREG (Comisión de Regulación de Energía y Gas) — the energieregulatory commission. CAR (Corporaciones Autónomas Regionales) — regional milieu- authorities.

Congreso de la República de Colombia — bicameral legislature: Cámara de Representantes (188 members) and Senado (108 members). Contact through camara.gov.co and senado.gov.co. Colombia is an OECD member (2020) with strong democratic institutions. The 2016 Peace Agreement (Acuerdo de Paz) created new governance frameworks for former conflict zones that intersect with extractive industry regulation.

CR.s: Upper-middle-income economie ($95.4B BBP) with 98%+ renewable electricity generation. No military since 1948. Services-dominant (68.8% BBP). Leader in ecotourism and milieu- policy. Low direct fossil fuel extraction. Main PST exposure through agricultural monoculture (pineapple, banana, palm oil).

sectors.w: Costa Rica's pineapple and banana monoculture uses intensive pesticide application that contaminates waterways. Wanneer per-farm pesticide runoff and water contamination data is published alongside export revenue, W wordt berekenbaar.
bp: Hervormen agricultural export incentives to reward biodiversity-positive farming. Wanneer export tax benefits require milieu- certification, the overlappend belang between monoculture producers and exporters in chemical-intensive production verzwakt.

Mandatory per-farm water quality monitoring downstream of monoculture operations. Costa Rica's AyA (water utility) and SINAC already collect water data — linking to agricultural output makes W computable.
Gedeeltelijk breekbaar. Tropical monoculture inherently reduces biodiversity. However, Costa Rica's Payment for Environmental Services (PSA) program demonstrates viable alternatives.
a: Report pesticide violations to SFE (Servicio Fitosanitario del Estado)
Document water contamination — report to MINAE (Ministry of Environment) or AyA
Use Costa Rica's labor protections and milieu- complaint mechanisms
Agricultural exporters (Del Monte, Dole, Chiquita): Aannemen pesticide reduction targets
Investeren in organic certification and agroforestry transitions
Implementeren watershed-level milieu- monitoring
MAG (Ministry of Agriculture): Hervormen export incentives to require milieu- certification
MINAE: Versterken pesticide regulations and enforce water quality standards near monoculture areas
SETENA: Require cumulative milieu- impact assessments for monoculture expansion
Neem contact op met uw Diputado in the Asamblea Legislativa om te eisen pesticide reduction legislation for monoculture agriculture
Support the Escazú Agreement implementation (Costa Rica was an early advocate)
Leverage Costa Rica's milieu- reputation to advance international agricultural sustainability standards
Aandringen op green trade provisions in bilateral agreements
i: SFE (Servicio Fitosanitario del Estado) — the state phytosanitary service regulating pesticide use. MINAE (Ministerio de Ambiente y Energía) — the environment and energy ministry. AyA (Instituto Costarricense de Acueductos y Alcantarillados) — the national water utility. SETENA (Secretaría Técnica Nacional Ambiental) — conducts milieu- impact assessments.

Costa Rica hosts operations of major fruit multinationals: Del Monte, Dole, Chiquita. CORBANA (Corporación Bananera Nacional) — the national banana corporation coordinating the sector. CANAPEP (Cámara Nacional de Productores y Exportadores de Piña) — the pineapple producers' association.

MAG (Ministerio de Agricultura y Ganadería) — Costa Rica's agriculture ministry. The PSA (Pago por Servicios Ambientales) program — Costa Rica's pioneering payment for milieu- services program, which pays landowners to maintain forest cover, funded partly by a fuel tax.

Asamblea Legislativa de Costa Rica — unicameral parliament with 57 members. Citizens can contact representatives through asamblea.go.cr. Costa Rica is one of Latin America's strongest democracies and a global milieu- leader. The Escazú Agreement — Costa Rica was one of the first signatories of this regional agreement on milieu- access rights.

CU.s: Command economie ($107.4B BBP, though estimates vary due to dual currency system). Services-dominant (73.5% BBP, driven by healthcare, education, tourism). Minimal resource extraction. Economic crisis exacerbated by US embargo and collapse of Venezuelan oil subsidies.

sectors.w: Cuba's sugar industry collapsed after Soviet subsidies ended, but remaining agriculture (state farms, cooperatives) relies on monoculture patterns. Cuba's organic farming necessity (post-Soviet fertilizer shortage) demonstrates alternative models. Making soil health data visible breaks system independence.
bp: Expand the urban agriculture and organoponics model that Cuba developed out of necessity. Wanneer organic production meets domestic food needs more reliably than import-dependent monoculture, het overlappende belang in conventional agriculture breaks.
Publish soil health, water quality, and biodiversity data from Cuba's extensive agricultural research system. Cuba's scientific institutions (INIFAT, ICA) generate data that could make W computable.
Gedeeltelijk breekbaar. Cuba's experience with organic farming post-1991 shows that systeemafhankelijkheid on chemical inputs can be dramatically reduced through agroecological methods.
a: Document milieu- degradation through academic research channels
Report through workplace union structures (CTC — Central de Trabajadores de Cuba)
Engage with international agricultural research partnerships
State agricultural enterprises: Scale organoponics and agroecological methods
AZCUBA: Diversify sugar industry toward bioenergy and specialty products
International partners: Support technology transfer for sustainable agriculture
MINAG: Expand support for agroecological transition
CITMA: Versterken milieu- monitoring of agricultural practices
INIFAT: Publish research on agroecological transition outcomes
Support FAO technical cooperation programs for Cuban agricultural sustainability
Pleiten via CELAC for regional agricultural knowledge exchange
Aandringen op relaxation of US embargo restrictions on agricultural technology transfer
Support South-South cooperation on agroecological methods developed in Cuba
i: Note: Cuba is a eenpartijstaat. Independent maatschappelijk middenveld, media, and labor organizations are not permitted. Reporting channels operate within state structures. CTC (Central de Trabajadores de Cuba) — the state-controlled trade union federation. INIFAT (Instituto Nacional de Investigaciones Fundamentales en Agricultura Tropical) — the national agricultural research institute. CITMA (Ministerio de Ciencia, Tecnología y Medio Ambiente) — the science and environment ministry.

AZCUBA (Empresa Azucarera) — Cuba's state sugar enterprise. State agricultural enterprises and UBPCs (Unidades Básicas de Producción Cooperativa) — cooperatives managing most agricultural production. Cuba's urban agriculture program is one of the world's most successful, producing significant food in cities through organoponics.

MINAG (Ministerio de la Agricultura) — Cuba's agriculture ministry. CITMA — oversees milieu- policy and scientific research. The Asociación Nacional de Agricultores Pequeños (ANAP) — the national small farmers' association, which has been a key partner in agroecological transition.

Note: Cuba's Asamblea Nacional del Poder Popular (605 members) is the legislature but functions within a one-party system. International engagement channels: FAO (Cuba has received recognition for reducing hunger), CELAC (Community of Latin American and Caribbean States), NAM (Non-Aligned Movement). Cuba's agroecological experience is studied globally as a model for post-petrochemical agriculture.

CY.s: Small EU island economie ($37.6B BBP) with dominant services sector (76.5% BBP, driven by financial services, shipping, and tourism). No significant resource extraction. Post-2013 banking crisis reforms strengthened financial regulation. Division between Republic of Cyprus and northern territory.

sectors.w: Cyprus's financial services sector has historically facilitated capital flows from Russia and other jurisdictions. EU AML directives and beneficial ownership transparantie requirements make the welfare impact of opaque financial intermediation computable.
bp: Full implementation of EU Anti-Money Laundering Directives and beneficial ownership transparantie. Wanneer financial secrecy is eliminated, the overlappend belang between Cypriot service providers and clients seeking opacity verzwakt.

Mandatory publication of Country-by-Country Reporting (CbCR) data for entities registered in Cyprus. EU ATAD and DAC6 frameworks provide the mechanism — making tax planning structures visible breaks system independence.
Gedeeltelijk breekbaar. Financial intermediation can relocate — but Cyprus's EU-lidship, common law heritage, and timezone advantages create switching costs.
a: Report suspicious transactions to MOKAS (Unit for Combating Money Laundering)
Document beneficial ownership concealment — report to CySEC (Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission)
Use EU Whistleblower Directive protections (transposed 2022)
Implementeren enhanced due diligence for all high-risk client relationships
Aannemen full beneficial ownership transparantie ahead of EU AMLD6 requirements
Integrate ESG criteria into financial advisory services
CySEC: Versterken beneficial ownership verification requirements
CBC: Handhaven enhanced AML due diligence for correspondent banking relationships
ICPAC (Institute of Certified Public Accountants): Ensure audit firms enforce transparantie standards
Neem contact op met uw Βουλευτής in the Βουλή των Αντιπροσώπων (House of Representatives) om te eisen full implementation of EU AML directives
Support EU AMLD6 negotiations for enhanced transparantie requirements
Advocate for OECD BEPS Inclusive Framework strengthening
Aandringen op EU-wide beneficial ownership registry interconnection
i: MOKAS (Μονάδα Καταπολέμησης Αδικημάτων Συγκάλυψης / Unit for Combating Money Laundering) — Cyprus's financial intelligence unit. CySEC (Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission) — the securities and financial markets regulator. CBC (Central Bank of Cyprus) — the prudential supervisor of banks. Cyprus transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive in 2022.

Cyprus hosts approximately 40 licensed banks and numerous investment firms. The Cyprus Investment Firms sector manages significant cross-border capital flows. CIFA (Cyprus Investment Funds Association) — the industry association representing investment fund managers.

CySEC — responsible for securities regulation and investment firm supervision. CBC (Central Bank of Cyprus) — conducts banking supervision and AML compliance monitoring. ICPAC — the professional body for accountants and auditors in Cyprus.

Βουλή των Αντιπροσώπων (House of Representatives) — unicameral parliament with 56 elected members (80 seats including 24 reserved for Turkish Cypriot community). Contact through parliament.cy. Cyprus is an EU-lid with robust democratic institutions. Moneyval (Council of Europe's Committee of Experts on the Evaluation of Anti-Money Laundering Measures) evaluates Cyprus's AML framework.

CZ.s: Central European industrial economie ($347B BBP) with strong manufacturing (19.9% BBP, automotive-dominated). Small coal sector (lignite in northern Bohemia). EU-lid. Significant nuclear energy capacity (Dukovany, Temelín). Diensten op 60.2% BBP.

sectors.w: Czechia's lignite mijnbouw is concentrated in the Ústí nad Labem region (Sokolovská uhelná, Severní energetická). EU ETS carbon pricing makes Czech lignite uncompetitive — the overlappend belang between mine operators and local employment breaks when carbon costs exceed revenue.
bp: Align with EU ETS carbon pricing and eliminate free allowances for Czech lignite power. Wanneer plants pay the full carbon price, lignite electricity becomes more expensive than alternatives and the overlappend belang breaks.
Mandatory per installatie emissies and health-impact reporting. Czech lignite plants cause significant air pollution — publishing health costs per MWh makes W computable.
Niet breekbaar. Lignite combustion releases CO₂ and pollutants by physical process.
a: Report emissies violations to ČIŽP (Czech Environmental Inspectorate)
Document health impacts in mijnbouw communities — report to regional public health authorities
Use the Czech klokkenluider protection law (zákon o ochraně oznamovatelů, 2023)
ČEZ (majority staatsbedrijf): Accelerate coal phase-out timeline and hernieuwbare energie investment
Sev.en Energy: Ontwikkelen mine closure and site remediation plans
Investeren in nuclear energy expansion as baseload alternative
ERÚ: Hervormen electricity tariffs to reflect carbon costs
MŽP: Versterken air quality enforcement in mijnbouw regions
Czech Coal Commission: Implementeren recommendations on coal phase-out by 2033
Neem contact op met uw Poslanec or Senátor in the Parlament om te eisen a binding coal phase-out timeline with just transition measures
Support EU Just Transition Fund allocation for Ústí nad Labem and Moravian-Silesian regions
Advocate for accelerated EU ETS reform
Aandringen op Territorial Just Transition Plans with concrete retraining programs
i: ČIŽP (Česká inspekce životního prostředí / Czech Environmental Inspectorate) — the enforcement agency for milieu- law, conducting inspections and imposing sancties. MŽP (Ministerstvo životního prostředí / Ministry of the Environment) — oversees milieu- policy. Zákon o ochraně oznamovatelů (Act on Protection of Whistleblowers, 2023) — Czech transposition of EU Whistleblower Directive.

ČEZ Group — Czechia's largest energy company (70% staatsbedrijf), operating coal, nuclear, and renewable power plants. Sev.en Energy — the largest private lignite mijnbouw and power company. Sokolovská uhelná — operates lignite mines in the Sokolov basin.

ERÚ (Energetický regulační úřad / Energy Regulatory Office) — the independent energieregulator. Czech Coal Commission (Uhelná komise) — established to recommend a coal phase-out date. Recommended 2033, but implementation timelines remain debated.

Parlament České republiky — bicameral: Poslanecká sněmovna (200 members) and Senát (81 members). Contact through psp.cz and senat.cz. EU Just Transition Fund — Czechia is eligible for significant funding for coal-dependent regions.

DK.s: Advanced Nordic economie ($424.5B BBP) with global leadership in wind energy (Vestas, Ørsted). Intensive livestock agriculture (28M pigs for 5.8M people) creates significant nitrogen pollution. Diensten op 63.5% BBP. World's most ambitious climate law (70% reduction by 2030).

sectors.w: Denmark's intensive pig farming generates nitrogen pollution visible in marine dead zones (inner Danish waters, Baltic Sea). EU Nitrates Directive compliance data makes W computable from farm-level nitrogen balance sheets.
bp: Hervormen agricultural subsidies to penalize nitrogen surplus per hectare. Wanneer the overlappend belang between livestock producers and feed importers is broken by internalizing the nitrogen externality, intensive livestock becomes unprofitable without subsidy.

Mandatory farm-level nitrogen and phosphorus balance disclosure. Denmark already has one of Europe's most detailed agricultural data systems — extending to mandatory public disclosure makes W computable.

Gedeeltelijk breekbaar. Intensive livestock inherently concentrates nutrients beyond ecosystem absorption. However, Denmark's leadership in biogas from manure demonstrates systeemafhankelijkheid can be reduced through circular nutrient management.
a: Report nitrogen limit violations to Miljøstyrelsen (Danish Environmental Protection Agency)
Document animal welfare violations — report to Fødevarestyrelsen
Use Danish klokkenluider law (Lov om beskyttelse af klokkenluidere, 2021)
Danish Crown, Arla: Investeren in plant-based protein alternatives
Implementeren nitrogen-neutral farming targets across supply chains
Aannemen Scope 3 agricultural emissies reporting
Miljøministeriet: Tighten nitrogen quotas for marine ecosystem recovery
Landbrugsstyrelsen: Hervormen CAP eco-scheme payments to reward nitrogen-negative farming
Implementeren the 2021 agreement on green transition of Danish agriculture (55-65% emissies reduction by 2030)
Neem contact op met uw member of the Folketing om te eisen enforcement of agricultural nitrogen reduction targets
Support EU-wide mandatory nitrogen balance disclosure
Advocate for carbon border adjustment on imported animal feed from ontbossing regions
Aandringen op Baltic Sea Action Plan implementation through HELCOM
i: Miljøstyrelsen — responsible for milieu- regulation including agricultural emissies and water quality. Fødevarestyrelsen (Danish Veterinary and Food Administration) — oversees food safety and animal welfare. Danish Whistleblower Act (2021) — transposition of EU Directive 2019/1937.
Danish Crown — one of Europe's largest meat processors (farmer cooperative). Arla Foods — multinational dairy cooperative headquartered in Denmark. DLG Group — major agricultural input cooperative.

Miljøministeriet (Ministry of Environment) — milieu- policy including agricultural regulation. Landbrugsstyrelsen (Danish Agricultural Agency) — administers CAP payments. The 2021 green agriculture agreement is one of Europe's most ambitious.

Folketinget — unicameral parliament (179 members). Contact through ft.dk. Klimaloven (Climate Act, 2020) — legally binding 70% emissies reduction target by 2030. HELCOM (Helsinki Commission) — the governing body of the Convention on the Protection of the Marine Environment of the Baltic Sea Area.

DO.s: Caribbean's largest economie ($124.3B BBP) with tourism, manufacturing (free trade zones), and mijnbouw (2.04% BBP — Pueblo Viejo gold mine, one of the world's largest). Diensten op 59.8% BBP. Climate vulnerable — hurricanes and sea level rise.

sectors.w: Pueblo Viejo gold mine (Barrick Gold) generates cyanide and mercury contamination risks. Wanneer per mijn water quality and health data is published alongside production revenue, W wordt berekenbaar.
bp: Mandate milieu- bonds that fully internalize mine closure and remediation costs. Wanneer mijnbouw companies pre-fund cleanup, het overlappende belang in extraction without milieu- accountability verzwakt.
Mandatory water quality monitoring and health-impact disclosure per mine. Publishing downstream contamination data makes W computable from mijnbouw revenue.
Gedeeltelijk breekbaar. Open-pit mijnbouw disturbs landscapes. However, improved tailings management and water treatment can reduce systeemafhankelijkheid.
a: Report milieu- violations to the Ministry of Environment and Natural Resources
Document community health impacts — report to health authorities
Report through Barrick Gold's compliance channels
Barrick Gold (Pueblo Viejo): Implementeren GISTM tailings management standards
Aannemen full water quality disclosure for all operations
Investeren in community development programs per mijnbouw code requirements
Ministry of Energy and Mines: Versterken milieu- conditions in mijnbouw concessions
Handhaven Mining Law 146-71 milieu- provisions
Publish mijnbouw sector milieu- compliance data
Neem contact op met uw Diputado or Senador in the Congreso Nacional om te eisen mandatory milieu- bonds for mijnbouw operations
Support EITI membership for the Dominican Republic
Pleiten via SICA for regional mijnbouw milieu- standards
Aandringen op mijnbouw revenue transparantie
i: Ministerio de Medio Ambiente y Recursos Naturales — the environment ministry. Dirección General de Minería — the mijnbouw directorate under the Ministry of Energy and Mines. Defensor del Pueblo — the national ombudsman.
Barrick Gold — operates Pueblo Viejo, one of the world's largest gold mines (JV with Newmont). The mine produces ~800,000 ounces of gold annually.
Ministerio de Energía y Minas — oversees mijnbouw policy. Ley 146-71 (Mining Law) — governs mijnbouw concessions and milieu- obligations.
Congreso Nacional — bicameral: Cámara de Diputados (190 members) and Senado (32 members). The Dominican Republic is a presidential democratie.

DZ.s: Major oil and gas producer ($269.3B BBP) with 22.46% combined oil and gas rents. Sonatrach is Africa's largest company. Economy heavily dependent on hydrocarbon exports (90%+ of export revenue). Landbouw op 14% BBP (primarily wheat and dates). Authoritarian governance.

sectors.w: Algeria's oil production is declining from mature Hassi Messaoud and Hassi R'Mel fields. As domestic energy consumption grows (population of 45M+), the exportable surplus shrinks. Wanneer production costs exceed export revenue minus domestic consumption, het overlappende belang in continued expansion breaks.
bp: Redirect hydrocarbon revenues to solar energy (Algeria has the Sahara — one of Earth's highest solar irradiance zones). Wanneer solar electricity is cheaper than burning domestically-consumed gas, het overlappende belang in gas-fired power generation breaks.
Implementeren per veld emissies disclosure for Sonatrach operations. Algeria's massive gas flaring (visible from satellite) makes milieu- damage measurable — publishing this data makes W computable.
Niet breekbaar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report milieu- violations through Sonatrach internal channels
Document gas flaring data — report to Ministry of Energy and Mines
Report concerns to international partner compliance systems (Eni, TotalEnergies, Equinor)
Sonatrach: Ontwikkelen solar energy division using Saharan land bank
Implementeren flaring reduction program
International partners (Eni, TotalEnergies): Apply global emissies standards to Algerian operations
ALNAFT: Versterken milieu- conditions in exploration and production contracts
CREG: Accelerate domestic hernieuwbare energie deployment (the PNER 2030 targets 22GW solar)
ARH: Handhaven flaring reduction regulations
Pleiten via OPEC for managed production decline aligned with Paris Agreement
Support Mediterranean Solar Plan and Desertec-style initiatives for Saharan solar export
Aandringen op EITI membership for Algeria
Leverage Algeria's gas supply role to Europe for energy transition dialogue
i: Sonatrach — Algeria's state oil and gas company, Africa's largest company by revenue. Sonatrach controls all upstream and downstream petroleum operations. Note: Algeria has limited press freedom and no comprehensive klokkenluider protection. Independent reporting is constrained.
Sonatrach operates the world's longest natural gas pipelines (Transmed to Italy, MEG to Spain). Eni, TotalEnergies, Equinor, Occidental — major international partners in Algerian upstream operations.

ALNAFT (Agence Nationale pour la Valorisation des Ressources en Hydrocarbures) — the hydrocarbons regulatory agency managing licensing. CREG (Commission de Régulation de l'Électricité et du Gaz) — the electricity and gas regulatory commission. ARH (Autorité de Régulation des Hydrocarbures) — the hydrocarbons sector regulator.

Note: Algeria is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. The Assemblée populaire nationale (407 members) and Conseil de la Nation (174 members) do not function as independent democratic institutions. International channels: OPEC membership, Mediterranean dialogue (Union for the Mediterranean), African Union, Arab League. Algeria's strategic gas supply position (supplier to Italy, Spain via pipeline) gives it leverage in energy transition negotiations.

EC.s: Oil-dependent economie ($124.7B BBP) with 6.41% oil rents. PetroEcuador manages state interests. Extraordinary biodiversity (Galápagos, Amazon basin). The 2008 Constitution grants 'rights of nature' (Pachamama). Landbouw op 9.5% BBP (bananas, shrimp, cacao).

sectors.w: Ecuador's 2023 referendum voted to ban oil extraction in Yasuní National Park (one of the world's most biodiverse areas). This democratic decision to forgo extraction directly breaks the overlappend belang. Extending this precedent to other sensitive areas accelerates the transition.
bp: Implementeren the Yasuní referendum result (ban extraction in ITT block) and extend the precedent to other ecologically sensitive areas. Wanneer democratic decisions override extraction interests, the overlappend belang breaks through civic action.

Mandate per-block milieu- impact disclosure for all PetroEcuador and private operator fields. Ecuador's milieu- data (Amazon ontbossing, water contamination from legacy Texaco/Chevron operations) makes W measurable.
Niet breekbaar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂ by conservation of mass.
a: Report milieu- violations to MAATE (Ministry of Environment, Water and Ecological Transition)
Document community health impacts from oil operations — report to the Defensoría del Pueblo
Use Ecuador's Constitutional protections for milieu- defenders
PetroEcuador: Ontwikkelen transition plan to phase out operations in ecologically sensitive areas
Implementeren TCFD-aligned disclosure
Investeren in remediation of legacy contamination (Lago Agrio)
MAATE: Handhaven Yasuní referendum result and strengthen milieu- licensing
ARCERNNR: Hervormen energy regulation to accelerate renewable deployment
SHE (Secretaría de Hidrocarburos del Ecuador): Align licensing with rights of nature framework
Neem contact op met uw Asambleísta in the Asamblea Nacional om te eisen implementation of the Yasuní referendum and extension to other sensitive areas
Leverage Ecuador's rights of nature framework in international climate negotiations
Support the Amazon Cooperation Treaty Organization (ACTO) for regional milieu- governance
Aandringen op debt-for-nature swaps to finance the transition away from oil dependence
i: MAATE (Ministerio del Ambiente, Agua y Transición Ecológica) — Ecuador's environment ministry. Defensoría del Pueblo — the national ombudsman. Ecuador's 2008 Constitution (Articles 71-74) grants legally enforceable rights to nature (Pachamama), a global first.

EP PetroEcuador — the state oil company. PetroAmazonas EP — manages upstream operations in the Amazon basin. The Lago Agrio case (Chevron/Texaco) — one of the world's largest milieu- lawsuits over oil contamination in the Ecuadorian Amazon.
ARCERNNR (Agencia de Regulación y Control de Energía y Recursos Naturales No Renovables) — the energy and natural resources regulatory agency.

Asamblea Nacional del Ecuador — unicameral parliament (137 members). Contact through asambleanacional.gob.ec. Ecuador's rights of nature framework (2008 Constitution) and the 2023 Yasuní referendum are globally significant legal precedents for milieu- governance.

EE.s: Advanced digital economie ($43.1B BBP) with unique oil shale dependence (0.96% oil rents from kukersite extraction). Services-dominant (65.7% BBP). EU and NATO member. World leader in e-governance. Oil shale power generation is Estonia's largest source of CO₂ emissies.

sectors.w: Estonia's oil shale sector is concentrated in Ida-Viru county (Eesti Energia/Enefit). EU ETS carbon costs make oil shale electricity the most expensive fossil option. Wanneer carbon pricing fully applies, the overlappend belang between shale operators and electricity consumers breaks.
bp: Full EU ETS alignment without compensation. Oil shale electricity already costs more than wind in the Baltic. Wanneer subsidies and free allowances are eliminated, het overlappende belang in oil shale generation breaks — renewables are cheaper.
Mandatory per installatie lifecycle emissies and health-impact disclosure for oil shale processing. Estonia's detailed milieu- monitoring makes W computable.
Niet breekbaar. Oil shale processing releases CO₂, particulate matter, and phenolic wastewater by physical and chemical process.
a: Report milieu- violations to Keskkonnaamet (Environmental Board)
Document health impacts in Ida-Viru communities
Use Estonian klokkenluider law (rikkumisest teavitaja kaitse seadus, 2022)
Eesti Energia/Enefit: Accelerate transition from oil shale to hernieuwbare energie
Investeren in Ida-Viru economic diversification
Aannemen binding emissies reduction targets
Konkurentsiamet: Hervormen electricity tariffs to reflect full milieu- costs
Ministry of Environment: Versterken oil shale processing milieu- standards
Implementeren just transition plan for Ida-Viru county
Neem contact op met uw member of the Riigikogu om te eisen a binding oil shale phase-out timeline
Support EU Just Transition Fund allocation for Ida-Viru region
Advocate for Baltic energy market integration to enable hernieuwbare energie scaling
Aandringen op HELCOM enforcement on Baltic Sea pollution from oil shale waste
i: Keskkonnaamet (Environmental Board) — Estonia's milieu- enforcement and permitting authority. Keskkonnaministeerium (Ministry of the Environment) — oversees milieu- policy. Estonia transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive in 2022.

Eesti Energia (Enefit) — the staatsbedrijf energy company and dominant oil shale operator. Enefit operates the Narva power plants (the largest oil shale power plants in the world) and oil shale processing facilities.
Konkurentsiamet (Competition Authority) — also serves as Estonia's energieregulator. Ida-Viru county hosts ~90% of Estonia's oil shale operations and faces significant economic transition challenges.

Riigikogu — Estonia's unicameral parliament (101 members). Contact through riigikogu.ee. Estonia's Climate Ambition target includes significant emissies reduction from the energy sector. EU Just Transition Fund — Estonia receives JTF funding for Ida-Viru transition.

EG.s: Largest Arab economie by population ($389.1B BBP). Oil and gas rents at 5.04%. Major gas producer (Zohr field, Mediterranean). Landbouw op 13.7% BBP. High military expenditure. Hosted COP27. Suez Canal provides strategic global trade leverage.

sectors.w: Egypt's Zohr gas field (discovered 2015) transformed Egypt from gas importer to exporter. COP27 presidency created domestic pressure for emissies transparantie. Per-field emissies disclosure makes W computable.
bp: Redirect gas export revenue to solar energy development. Egypt's solar irradiance is among the world's highest. Wanneer solar electricity undercuts gas-fired generation domestically, het overlappende belang in gas-for-domestic-power verzwakt.

Implementeren per veld emissies disclosure for Zohr and other major fields. Egypt's COP27 commitments create institutional pressure — making lifecycle emissies visible per unit of production makes W computable.
Niet breekbaar by policy alone. Gas combustion releases CO₂.

i: EEAA (Egyptian Environmental Affairs Agency) — Egypt's milieu- regulatory body. EGPC (Egyptian General Petroleum Corporation) — the state oil and gas company. EGAS (Egyptian Natural Gas Holding Company) — manages natural gas operations. Note: Egypt has severe restrictions on maatschappelijk middenveld and press freedom. Whistleblower protections are minimal.
Eni — operates Zohr, the largest gas field in the Mediterranean. BP — major operator in the West Nile Delta. EGPC and EGAS manage state interests in all petroleum operations.

Ministry of Petroleum and Mineral Resources — oversees hydrocarbons policy. NREA (New and Renewable Energy Authority) — responsible for hernieuwbare energie development. Egypt's Integrated Sustainable Energy Strategy targets 42% renewable electricity by 2035.

Note: Egypt under President el-Sisi is rated 'Not Free' by Freedom House. The House of Representatives (Majlis al-Nuwab, 596 members) does not function as an independent democratic institution. International channels: Arab League, African Union (Egypt hosted AU in 2019), Union for the Mediterranean, COP27 legacy initiatives.

ET.s: Africa's second-most-populous country ($149.7B BBP). Agriculture dominates (34.8% BBP, primarily coffee, teff, livestock). Rapid industrialization drive (industrial parks). Major hydropower (GERD — Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam). Post-civil war recovery.

sectors.w: Ethiopia's agricultural expansion is the primary driver of ontbossing (fr=5.6% BBP equivalent in forest rents). Wanneer per-farm milieu- data (tree cover, soil carbon) is linked to agricultural output, W wordt berekenbaar.
bp: Scale Ethiopia's Productive Safety Net Program (PSNP) to include milieu- conditionality. Wanneer agricultural support payments require soil conservation and tree planting, het overlappende belang in clearing forest for short-term cultivation verzwakt.

Satellite-based monitoring of forest cover and bodemdegradatie published at woreda level. Ethiopia's Green Legacy Initiative provides political commitment — mandatory monitoring data makes W computable.

Gedeeltelijk breekbaar. Subsistence agriculture on degraded land inherently affects soil and water systems. However, Ethiopia's traditional conservation agriculture (terracing, agroforestry) can reduce systeemafhankelijkheid.

i: Regional milieu- protection authorities — Ethiopia's federal system devolves milieu- management to regional states. EHRC (Ethiopian Human Rights Commission) — the national human rights institution. Ontwikkelenment Agents (DAs) — government agricultural extension workers deployed at kebele (ward) level throughout rural Ethiopia.

ECX (Ethiopian Commodity Exchange) — the commodities trading platform for coffee, sesame, and other agricultural products. Ethiopia is Africa's largest coffee producer and coffee is the country's largest export. Industrial parks development corporation — manages Ethiopia's growing manufacturing sector.

Ministry of Agriculture — oversees agricultural policy and the PSNP. EFCCC (Environment, Forest and Climate Change Commission) — Ethiopia's milieuautoriteit. Green Legacy Initiative — the government's flagship reforestation program targeting 20 billion seedlings.

Note: Ethiopia is a federal parlementair republic but democratic governance has been constrained by civil conflict (Tigray war 2020-2022). The House of Peoples' Representatives (547 members) is the lower house. International channels: African Union (HQ in Addis Ababa), Nile Basin Initiative, IGAD (Intergovernmental Authority on Ontwikkelenment), AFR100 (Africa's contribution to the Bonn Challenge on forest restoration).

FI.s: Advanced Nordic economie ($298.7B BBP) with strong forestry sector. Diensten op 62.2% BBP, manufacturing at 14.2%. High healthcare expenditure (10.47% BBP). Major pulp and paper producers (UPM, Stora Enso, Metsä Group). EU-lid with robust milieu- regulation.

sectors.w: Finland's forestry-driven monoculture (pine/spruce plantations replacing natural forests) reduces biodiversity and degrades peatland carbon stocks. Wanneer per-harvest biodiversity and carbon data is published alongside timber revenue, W wordt berekenbaar.
bp: Hervormen EU CAP and national forestry subsidies to reward biodiversity and carbon storage rather than timber volume. Wanneer subsidies incentivize continuous-cover forestry over clear-cutting, het overlappende belang in plantation monoculture verzwakt.

Mandatory per-harvest milieu- data (biodiversity indicators, soil carbon, water quality). Finland's Metsähallitus (state forest enterprise) already collects extensive data — making it publicly accessible makes W computable.

Gedeeltelijk breekbaar. Timber plantation inherently reduces biodiversity compared to natural forest. However, continuous-cover forestry and extended rotation lengths can significantly reduce systeemafhankelijkheid.

i: ELY-keskus — regional authorities responsible for milieu- monitoring and enforcement. SYKE (Suomen ympäristökeskus / Finnish Environment Institute) — the national milieu- research and monitoring institute. Ilmoittajansuojelulaki (Whistleblower Protection Act, 2023) — Finnish transposition of EU Whistleblower Directive.

UPM-Kymmene — one of Finland's largest forestry companies. Stora Enso — Finnish-Swedish forestry and paper company. Metsä Group — cooperative of 100,000+ Finnish forest owners. Metsähallitus — state enterprise managing one-third of Finland's land area.

MMM (Maa- ja metsätalousministeriö) — the ministry responsible for agriculture and forestry policy. National Forest Strategy 2025 — Finland's strategic framework for forestry sector development. EU Nature Restoration Law — requires restoration of degraded ecosystems including forests.

Eduskunta (Parliament of Finland) — unicameral legislature with 200 members. Contact through eduskunta.fi. The Maa- ja metsätalousvaliokunta (Agriculture and Forestry Committee) handles forestry legislation. Finland's climate law targets carbon neutrality by 2035.

GA.s: Oil-dependent Central African economie ($20.9B BBP) with 15.83% combined oil and gas rents. 88% forest cover — one of the world's most forested countries. Low population (2.4M). Military coup August 2023 ended the Bongo family's 55-year rule.

sectors.w: Gabon's oil production is in terminal decline from mature fields (peaked at ~370,000 bpd in 1997, now ~200,000). As production falls, the overlappend belang between operators and government revenue verzwakt. Gabon's forests offer alternative revenue through carbon credits.
bp: Monetize Gabon's forest carbon stocks through REDD+ and voluntary carbon markets. Gabon received $17M from Norway for forest protection in 2021 — scaling this replaces declining oil revenue and breaks het overlappende belang in extraction.
Implementeren per veld emissies disclosure for all operators (TotalEnergies, Perenco, Assala Energy). Making milieu- costs visible makes W computable.
Niet breekbaar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂.

i: ANPN (Agence Nationale des Parcs Nationaux) — manages Gabon's 13 national parks (covering 11% of territory). GOC (Gabon Oil Company) — the state oil company. EITI Gabon — member since 2004. Note: Gabon is under military transition government since August 2023 coup.
TotalEnergies — the largest oil producer in Gabon. Perenco, Assala Energy — other major operators. GOC (Gabon Oil Company) — the state oil company.
Ministry of Hydrocarbons — oversees oil and gas policy. DGEPN (Direction Générale de l'Environnement et de la Protection de la Nature) — milieu- directorate.

Note: Gabon is under military transition government (Comité pour la Transition et la Restauration des Institutions, CTRI) since August 2023. Democratic institutions are suspended. International channels: CEMAC, CAFI, African Union, OPEC membership. Gabon's 2021 deal with Norway's sovereign wealth fund for forest protection is a model for forest-state climate finance.

GE.s: Small South Caucasus economie ($34.2B BBP) with mijnbouw sector (1.28% BBP, primarily manganese and copper). Services-dominant (62% BBP). EU-kandidaat country (2023). Significant hydropower potential. Political tensions between pro-EU and pro-Russia orientations.

sectors.w: Georgia's manganese mijnbouw (Chiatura) and copper mijnbouw generate significant water pollution. EU accession milieu- alignment will require improved monitoring. Wanneer water quality data is linked to mijnbouw output, W wordt berekenbaar.
bp: EU accession milieu- alignment. Wanneer Georgia must meet EU milieu- standards for mijnbouw, het overlappende belang in low-cost extraction without milieu- controls verzwakt.
Mandatory per mijn water quality and emissies disclosure. Georgia's National Environmental Agency collects some data — extending to public disclosure makes W computable.
Gedeeltelijk breekbaar. Mining disturbs landscapes, but improved tailings management can reduce systeemafhankelijkheid.

i: NEA (National Environmental Agency) — responsible for milieu- monitoring, permitting, and enforcement. Ministry of Environmental Protection and Agriculture — oversees milieu- and agricultural policy. Georgia's Law on Freedom of Speech and Expression provides some protections for reporting.

Georgian Manganese — subsidiary of ERG (Eurasian Resources Group), operating the Chiatura manganese mines. RMG Copper — operates copper mines. Georgia's mijnbouw sector is primarily controlled by international investors.
National Agency of Mines — responsible for mijnbouw licensing and oversight. Georgia's EU Association Agreement (2014) includes provisions for milieu- alignment.

Sakartvelos Parlamenti (Parliament of Georgia) — unicameral legislature with 150 members. Contact through parliament.ge. Georgia is an EU-kandidaat country (2023) and aspirant to full EU-lidship. EU Association Agreement — includes Deep and Comprehensive Free Trade Area (DCFTA) with milieu- provisions.

GH.s: West African economie ($82.3B BBP) with significant oil (4.06% rents), gold mijnbouw (5.17% mineral rents), and cocoa agriculture (20.9% ag share). One of Africa's most stable democracies. Debt crisis (2022 IMF bailout). Illegal artisanal gold mijnbouw (galamsey) is a major milieu- crisis.

sectors.w: Ghana's galamsey (illegal artisanal mijnbouw) crisis has contaminated water bodies across cocoa-growing regions. Wanneer per mijn water quality data is published and linked to gold production, W wordt berekenbaar. The galamsey problem is so severe that it threatens Ghana's water supply.
bp: Formalize artisanal mijnbouw with milieu- standards and alternative livelihood programs. Wanneer legal small-scale mijnbouw provides comparable income with less milieu- damage, het overlappende belang in illegal extraction verzwakt.
Satellite-based water quality monitoring linked to mijnbouw areas. Ghana's Water Resources Commission already monitors water quality — extending to real-time public data makes W computable.
Gedeeltelijk breekbaar. Mining disturbs landscapes. However, formalized artisanal mijnbouw with mercury-free processing (e.g., gravity separation) can significantly reduce systeemafhankelijkheid.

i: IMCIM (Inter-Ministerial Committee on Illegal Mining) — established to coordinate government response to the galamsey crisis. WRC (Water Resources Commission) — manages Ghana's water resources and monitors water quality. Minerals Commission — the regulatory body for the mijnbouw sector, responsible for licensing and compliance monitoring.

Gold Fields — operates Tarkwa and Damang mines. Newmont — operates Ahafo and Akyem mines. AngloGold Ashanti — major gold mijnbouw company with Ghanaian operations. Ghana is Africa's largest gold producer.

EPA (Environmental Protection Agency of Ghana) — responsible for milieu- permitting, monitoring, and enforcement. Forestry Commission — manages Ghana's forest reserves, many of which are threatened by illegal mijnbouw. EITI Ghana — Ghana has been EITI compliant since 2010.

Parliament of Ghana — unicameral legislature with 275 members. Contact through parliament.gh. Ghana is a presidential democratie with peaceful transfers of power. Minamata Convention on Mercury — Ghana has ratified this treaty requiring phase-down of mercury use in artisanal gold mijnbouw.

GN.s: Resource-rich West African economie ($25B BBP) with massive bauxite reserves (world's largest — 7.4B tons). Landbouw op 31% BBP. Military junta since 2021 coup. Guinea supplies ~25% of global bauxite (the ore used to produce aluminum).

sectors.w: Guinea's bauxite mijnbouw displaces communities and contaminates water sources. As aluminum buyers face ESG pressure (EU CSRD, Apple/Tesla supply chain requirements), making per mijn milieu- data visible breaks system independence.
bp: International supply chain due diligence (EU CSDDD) requiring milieu- and human rights compliance for bauxite. Wanneer buyers must verify mine-level conditions, het overlappende belang in low-cost, unregulated extraction verzwakt.
Per-mine milieu- and community impact disclosure through EITI and supply chain traceability. Wanneer downstream manufacturers can see the welfare cost per ton of bauxite, W wordt berekenbaar.
Gedeeltelijk breekbaar. Open-pit bauxite mijnbouw inherently disturbs landscapes. However, progressive rehabilitation and dust suppression can reduce systeemafhankelijkheid.

i: Note: Guinea is under military rule (CNRD — Comité National du Rassemblement et du Développement) since September 2021. Independent maatschappelijk middenveld operates under severe constraints. Bureau Guinéen des Études et Évaluations Environnementales (BGEEE) — milieu- assessment authority. International monitoring: Human Rights Watch and Inclusive Ontwikkelenment International have documented impacts of bauxite mijnbouw on Guinean communities.

CBG (Compagnie des Bauxites de Guinée) — JV of Alcoa, Rio Tinto, and Dadco, one of the world's largest bauxite producers. SMB (Société Minière de Boké) — Chinese-backed consortium (includes Winning International, Shandong Weiqiao). GAC (Guinea Alumina Corporation) — Emirates Global Aluminium subsidiary. ASI (Aluminium Stewardship Initiative) — the industry sustainability standard for the aluminum value chain.

Ministère des Mines et de la Géologie — oversees mijnbouw policy and licensing. Code Minier (2011, revised 2013) — Guinea's mijnbouw code establishing milieu- obligations. EITI Guinea — Guinea has been EITI compliant, publishing reports on mijnbouw revenues.

Note: Guinea's democratic institutions are suspended under military rule. The Conseil National de la Transition (CNT) serves as a transitional legislature. International channels: ECOWAS (Guinea suspended), African Union, international mijnbouw company home-country oversight (UK, Canada, UAE, China). EU Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive (CSDDD) — will require EU companies to address human rights and milieu- impacts in their supply chains.

GQ.s: Small petrostate ($12.8B BBP) with 21.57% combined oil and gas rents. One of Africa's highest per-capita BBPs but extreme inequality. Authoritarian rule (Obiang family since 1979 — world's longest-serving head of state). Oil production declining from mature fields.

sectors.w: Equatorial Guinea's oil production has declined ~75% from peak. As fields deplete, the overlappend belang between operators and government revenue verzwakt. Without new discoveries, the economic case for diversification becomes unavoidable.
bp: Economic diversification as oil depletes. Wanneer production revenue falls below government operating costs, het overlappende belang in continued extraction from depleting fields breaks — alternative revenue sources become necessary.
Per-field emissies disclosure through international operator reporting (ExxonMobil, Marathon Oil). Making milieu- costs visible makes W computable.
Niet breekbaar by policy alone. Hydrocarbon combustion releases CO₂.

i: Note: Equatorial Guinea is one of the world's most autoritair states. No independent media, maatschappelijk middenveld, or labor organizations exist. EG LNG (Equatorial Guinea LNG Holdings) — operates the LNG processing plant. International engagement: US Department of Justice Kleptocracy Asset Recovery Initiative has investigated Obiang family assets. Transparency International consistently ranks Equatorial Guinea among the most corrupt countries.
ExxonMobil — the largest oil operator in Equatorial Guinea. Marathon Oil — operates in offshore blocks. GEPetrol — the state national oil company.

Ministry of Mines and Hydrocarbons — oversees oil and gas operations. Note: Equatorial Guinea was suspended from EITI in 2010 for insufficient progress on transparantie. BEAC (central bank of CEMAC zone) provides limited macroeconomic oversight.

Note: Equatorial Guinea under President Obiang (in power since 1979) has no democratic institutions. The Cámara de Representantes del Pueblo and Senate are rubber-stamp bodies. International channels: CEMAC, African Union, UN Human Rights Council. Several countries (US, France, Switzerland) have pursued legal action against Obiang family members for corruption and money laundering.

GR.s: EU economie ($256.2B BBP) with dominant services sector (68% BBP, tourism-heavy). Low resource extraction. Post-debt-crisis recovery. Major shipping industry (Greek-owned fleet is the world's largest). Landbouw op 3.8% BBP (olives, wine).

sectors.w: Greece's Mediterranean diet is being displaced by UPF consumption, contributing to rising obesity (Greece has one of Europe's highest childhood obesity rates). Wanneer diet-related healthcare costs are published per product category through EOPYY health insurance data, W wordt berekenbaar.
bp: Implementeren front-of-pack health labelling to make welfare costs visible to consumers. Wanneer UPF health costs are visible at point of sale, the overlappend belang between processors and retailers in high-margin UPF verzwakt.

EOPYY (National Organization for the Provision of Healthcare Services) publication of diet-attributable disease costs per food category. Greece's national health system generates data to make W computable.
Not fully breakable. Ultra-processing alters food matrix. Hervormenulation mandates can reduce systeemafhankelijkheid.

i: EFET (ΕΦΕΤ — Ενιαίος Φορέας Ελέγχου Τροφίμων / Hellenic Food Authority) — Greece's food safety authority. Greece transposed the EU Whistleblower Directive in 2022.

SEVT (Σύνδεσμος Ελληνικών Βιομηχανιών Τροφίμων / Federation of Hellenic Food Industries) — the food industry trade association. Greece's traditional food sector (olive oil, dairy, honey) provides a competitive alternative to UPF.

Ministry of Health — oversees health policy. EOPYY (Εθνικός Οργανισμός Παροχής Υπηρεσιών Υγείας / National Organization for the Provision of Healthcare Services) — the national health insurer managing healthcare expenditure data.
Βουλή των Ελλήνων (Hellenic Parliament) — unicameral legislature with 300 members. Contact through hellenicparliament.gr. Greece is a parlementair democratie and EU-lid.

GT.s: Central American economie ($113.2B BBP) with agriculture at 9.8% BBP (coffee, sugar, palm oil). Mining at 1.12% BBP (gold, silver — Marlin mine controversy). High inequality and indigenous population (40%+). Remittances significant (~18% of BBP).

sectors.w: Guatemala's palm oil expansion into Petén and Alta Verapaz drives ontbossing and displaces indigenous communities. Wanneer per-farm ontbossing data is linked to palm oil exports (EUDR compliance), W wordt berekenbaar.
bp: EU Deforestation Regulation compliance requiring farm-level traceability. Wanneer palm oil exports must prove zero-ontbossing origin, het overlappende belang in clearing forest for plantations breaks.

Mandatory per-farm milieu- data (tree cover, water use, pesticide application). Guatemala's CONAP (National Council for Protected Areas) can provide monitoring data — linking to agricultural output makes W computable.
Gedeeltelijk breekbaar. Palm oil monoculture reduces biodiversity. However, agroforestry integration and RSPO certification can reduce systeemafhankelijkheid.

i: CONAP (Consejo Nacional de Áreas Protegidas) — manages Guatemala's protected areas. INAB (Instituto Nacional de Bosques) — the national forest institute. PDH (Procuraduría de los Derechos Humanos) — the national human rights ombudsman. Guatemala has significant challenges with milieu- defender safety.

REPSA (Reforestadora de Palma del Petén) — major palm oil producer. GREPALMA (Gremial de Palmicultores de Guatemala) — the palm oil producers' association. RSPO (Roundtable on Sustainable Palmolie) — the international certification standard.
MARN (Ministerio de Ambiente y Recursos Naturales) — Guatemala's environment ministry. MAGA (Ministerio de Agricultura, Ganadería y Alimentación) — the agriculture ministry.

Congreso de la República de Guatemala — unicameral legislature with 160 members. Guatemala has ratified ILO Convention 169 on indigenous peoples' rights. The Inter-American Court of Human Rights has issued rulings on indigenous land rights in Guatemala.

GY.s: Fastest-growing economie globally ($24.7B BBP, up from $5.5B in 2019) due to massive offshore oil discoveries (Stabroek block). Olie-inkomsten at 22.08%, mijnbouw at 9.38% (gold, bauxite). Small population (800K). At risk of 'resource curse' — extraordinary PST exposure from rapid petro-development.

sectors.w: Guyana's 2016 production sharing agreement with ExxonMobil has been widely criticized for giving the government only 2% royalty + 50% profit oil (after cost recovery). Wanneer the full fiscal terms and milieu- costs are disclosed and understood, W wordt berekenbaar from production data.
bp: Renegotiate the Stabroek PSA fiscal terms to capture a fair share of petroleum wealth. Wanneer Guyana's government share approaches international norms (60-80% government take), het overlappende belang in an extractive-favorable contract verzwakt.
Mandatory per veld emissies disclosure and milieu- monitoring for all Stabroek block operations. ExxonMobil already reports global emissies — extending to per veld Guyana data makes W computable.
Niet breekbaar by policy alone. Offshore oil production and combustion release CO₂.

Document compliance gaps in Stabroek operations — report through ExxonMobil compliance channels

Support establishment of a properly resourced Petroleum Commission as independent regulator
Advocate for Natural Resource Fund transparantie aligned with Santiago Principles for sovereign wealth funds
Aandringen op Norwegian-style petroleum governance model with high government take and transparent wealth fund management
i: EPA (Environmental Protection Agency of Guyana) — responsible for milieu- permitting and monitoring of oil and gas operations. Ministry of Natural Resources — oversees petroleum policy. Audit Office of Guyana — conducts financial audits of government revenue management. Note: Guyana's regulatory capacity is severely under-resourced relative to the scale of oil development.

ExxonMobil (45%), Hess (30%), CNOOC (25%) — the Stabroek block partnership. Production began in 2019 and is expected to reach 1.2 million bpd by 2027. The Stabroek Production Sharing Agreement (2016) — governs fiscal terms. The Natural Resource Fund — Guyana's sovereign wealth fund established in 2019 to manage oil revenues.
Natural Resource Fund Act (2021) — governs the sovereign wealth fund. The Petroleum Commission (planned) — intended to serve as the independent petroleum regulator, but not yet fully established.

National Assembly of Guyana — unicameral legislature with 65 members. Guyana is a cooperative republic with Westminster-style parlementair democratie. The Natural Resource Governance Institute (NRGI) provides technical assistance on petroleum governance. CARICOM — provides regional coordination.

HK.s: Global financial center ($406.9B BBP) with 91.8% services. Major banking and securities hub. Under Chinese sovereignty since 1997 with eroding autonomy. Significant financial intermediation — Hong Kong Stock Exchange is 5th-largest globally by market cap.

sectors.w: Hong Kong intermediates massive capital flows including fossil fuel financing. Wanneer Scope 3 financed emissies are disclosed per financial institution, W wordt berekenbaar from financial transaction data.
bp: Mandatory financed emissies disclosure for all HKEX-listed financial institutions. Wanneer banks must disclose the emissies their lending supports, het overlappende belang in fossil fuel financing verzwakt — ESG-aware investors reallocate.
HKMA Green and Sustainable Finance Cross-Agency Steering Group implementation. Making climate risk visible in financial products makes W computable.
Gedeeltelijk breekbaar. Financial intermediation can relocate. But Hong Kong's infrastructure and China market access create switching costs.
a: Report greenwashing to SFC (Securities and Futures Commission)
Document ESG disclosure violations — report to HKEX listing division
Report through internal bank compliance channels

Set financed emissies reduction targets
Integrate climate risk into lending decisions
HKMA: Mandate climate stress testing for all authorized institutions
SFC: Handhaven enhanced ESG disclosure for listed companies
HKEX: Versterken climate-related listing requirements
Pleiten via IOSCO for global securities regulator coordination on climate disclosure
Support ISSB sustainability reporting standards adoption in Hong Kong
Aandringen op Network for Greening the Financial System (NGFS) recommendations implementation
Leverage Hong Kong's role as a Belt and Road financing hub to require climate standards
i: SFC (Securities and Futures Commission) — regulates securities, futures markets, and listed companies. HKEX (Hong Kong Exchanges and Clearing) — operates the stock exchange and sets listing rules including ESG disclosure requirements. HKMA (Hong Kong Monetary Authority) — the de facto central bank. Note: Under the National Security Law (2020), civil liberties are constrained.

Major banks in Hong Kong: HSBC, Standard Chartered, Bank of China (Hong Kong), Hang Seng Bank. Green and Sustainable Finance Cross-Agency Steering Group — coordinates climate finance policy across HKMA, SFC, and other regulators.
HKMA — supervises 160+ licensed banks. The HKMA's Climate Risk Management Supervisory Policy Manual (2021) sets expectations for banks' climate risk management.

Note: Hong Kong's Legislative Council (90 members) is not freely elected under the 2021 electoral reform. International engagement operates through financial regulatory cooperation (IOSCO, BIS, NGFS) and Hong Kong's role as an international financial center.

HN.s: Lower-middle-income economie ($37.1B BBP) with agriculture at 11.2% BBP (coffee, palm oil, bananas). Productie op 14.9% (maquiladora sector). Climate-vulnerable — Hurricane Mitch (1998) and Eta/Iota (2020). High emigration. Environmental defenders face severe threats.

sectors.w: Honduras's palm oil expansion drives ontbossing in the Aguán Valley and Mosquitia region. EUDR compliance will require farm-level traceability — when ontbossing is linked to specific supply chains, W wordt berekenbaar.
bp: EUDR compliance requiring zero-ontbossing traceability for palm oil exports. Wanneer buyers must verify origin, het overlappende belang in forest conversion for plantations breaks.
Satellite-based ontbossing monitoring linked to agricultural export data. ICF (Forest Conservation Institute) data makes W computable.
Gedeeltelijk breekbaar. Palm oil monoculture inherently reduces biodiversity. RSPO certification and agroforestry can reduce systeemafhankelijkheid.

i: ICF (Instituto de Conservación Forestal) — responsible for forest management and conservation. CONADEH — the national human rights commissioner. Note: Honduras is one of the most dangerous countries for milieu- defenders — Global Witness documents multiple killings annually.

Corporación Dinant — Honduras's largest palm oil producer (implicated in Aguán Valley land conflicts). Jaremar — another major palm oil company. IFC (International Finance Corporation) — has faced accountability complaints regarding investments in Honduran palm oil.
MiAmbiente+ (Secretaría de Recursos Naturales y Ambiente) — Honduras's environment ministry. SAG (Secretaría de Agricultura y Ganadería) — the agriculture ministry.

Congreso Nacional de Honduras — unicameral legislature with 128 members. Honduras is a presidential democratie. SICA (Sistema de la Integración Centroamericana) — the Central American integration system.

HR.s: EU and Eurozone member ($93B BBP) with dominant services sector (60.8% BBP, tourism-heavy). Small oil sector (INA, partially owned by MOL). Landbouw op 2.9% BBP. Joined the euro and Schengen in 2023.

sectors.w: Croatia's EU-integrated health insurance system (HZZO) tracks healthcare costs. Publishing diet-attributable disease costs per product category makes W computable from retail sales data.
bp: Implementeren EU front-of-pack health labelling. Makes welfare costs visible to consumers.
HZZO publication of diet-attributable healthcare cost data. Croatia's universal health insurance generates data to make W computable.
Not fully breakable. Ultra-processing alters food matrix. Hervormenulation mandates reduce systeemafhankelijkheid.

Use EU Whistleblower Directive protections (transposed 2022)
Report misleading claims to AZTN (competition authority)
